prompt_text: Open source or affordable customer support tools instead of Drift
response:
You can replace Drift with either **open‑source, self‑hosted** tools (no per‑seat SaaS fees, but more setup) or **affordable SaaS** tools with generous free/low‑cost plans. Below are strong options in both categories.

---

## 1. Open‑source, self‑hosted Drift‑style options

These give you live chat + shared inbox/help desk, and you host them yourself.

### Chatwoot

- **What it is:** Modern, open‑source customer support & engagement platform; explicit alternative to Intercom/Zendesk (similar to Drift’s use case).[2][3]
- **Key features:**
  - Website **live chat widget**
  - **Omnichannel inbox**: email, website, Facebook, Instagram, Telegram, Line, and more in one place[2][3]
  - Team inbox, assignments, canned responses, CSAT, basic automations
  - Knowledge base, basic AI assistant options[2]
- **Why it’s a good Drift alternative:**
  - Very similar mental model: conversations, inboxes, tags, contact profiles.
  - Open source and **self‑hostable**, so you own the data and avoid per‑agent pricing.[2]
- **Trade‑offs:** Requires server + basic DevOps to deploy and maintain; no native product tours or advanced “playbooks” like Drift out of the box.

---

### FreeScout

- **What it is:** Lightweight, free **open‑source help desk and shared inbox**.[4]
- **Key features:**
  - Email‑based ticketing (connect your support@ inbox; emails become tickets)[4]
  - Extensions for live chat, CRM integration, SMS via Twilio, etc.[1][4]
  - Runs on cheap PHP hosting; very small footprint (can even run on shared hosting).[4]
- **Why use it:**
  - Ideal if most of your “Drift” use is actually email + simple chat rather than complex sales playbooks.
  - Very low infrastructure requirements and highly affordable.
- **Trade‑offs:** More email/ticket‑centered than “engagement”‑centered; add‑ons are paid, but still cheap compared with SaaS per‑seat pricing.

---

### osTicket

- **What it is:** One of the oldest and most widely used open‑source **support ticket systems**.[1][4]
- **Key features:**
  - Central ticketing from email, phone, and web forms[1]
  - Custom fields, custom queues, ticket filters, email autoresponders[1]
- **Why use it:**
  - Rock‑solid, simple ticketing if you mostly need structured support rather than proactive website chat.
- **Trade‑offs:** UI is dated; not focused on modern live chat or sales engagement.

---

### Zammad

- **What it is:** Open‑source **multichannel help desk** and ticketing system.[1][4]
- **Key features:**
  - Email, chat, social media, phone in one system[1]
  - Modern UI, multilingual support (up to 40 languages) and third‑party integrations[1]
- **Why use it:**
  - More modern and feature‑rich than osTicket; good if you want omnichannel inbox plus chat.
- **Trade‑offs:** Heavier stack (Ruby), more complex to host than simpler PHP tools like FreeScout.

---

### Other notable open‑source help desks

Summarized options from open‑source roundups:[1][4][8]

- **Faveo Helpdesk** – Laravel‑based, customizable omni‑channel help desk (ticketing, email, some chat).[4][8]
- **UVdesk** – Open‑source help desk targeted at **e‑commerce** and marketplaces.[1][4]
- **Helpy** – Open‑source help desk with knowledge base and email‑to‑ticket; also has a hosted “Helpy Pro” option if you don’t want to self‑manage.[5]

---

## 2. Affordable hosted (SaaS) customer support tools

If you want something closer to Drift’s UX but don’t want to manage servers, these are typically cheaper than Drift and have free/low tiers (not all are open source):

- **Nextiva** – Full help desk/contact center suite; plans *from about \$15/user/month* and an “all features” plan around \$75.[1] More call‑center oriented but still a budget option compared with high‑end tools.
- **Various free/cheap SaaS tools** – Roundups of free customer service software list tools with live chat, shared inbox, and knowledge base on free tiers, which can be much cheaper than Drift for small teams.[7][9] (Examples in those lists include Help Scout alternatives, basic Zendes
